notes: Bumped after v0.97.2..v0.98.1 sweep (release 0.98.1; note v0.98.0 was withdrawn/pre-release — its web container image failed to build, so v0.98.1 is the first deployable tag of that range). UPDATED: deploy/environment.mdx (new 'Customer Microsoft 365 Graph-actions (write) executor (optional)' section — 9 vars, all optional/off-by-default behind M365_GRAPH_ACTIONS_TOOLS_ENABLED=false, mirrors the existing Graph-read section incl. separately-deployed digest-pinned sidecar + compose-mapping caution); features/ai.mdx (Approval workflow: new '#### Who can approve' documenting the approvals:decide permission — default Org Admin + Partner Admin, deliberately NOT Org Technician/Viewer, auto-cancel when no eligible approver — and '#### Pending approvals survive a restart' covering durable Tier-3 intents + headless Google/M365 identity execution post-approval); features/quotes.mdx (Sending and Tracking: ~30s 'Sending in Ns — Undo' window; explicitly NOT future-dated scheduling — the send_scheduled_at column backs the undo window only, max delay 300s, quote stays draft). mapping.json: +8 patterns (m365ControlPlane/writeActionRuntimeConfig.ts, writeActionService.ts, graphActionsExecutorClient.ts, services/actionIntents/**, actionIntents/intentApprovers.ts, googleToolsHeadless.ts, m365ToolsHeadless.ts, jobs/quoteSendQueue.ts). SKIPPED (audience filter, no user-facing surface): runtime extension platform #2617/#2633/#2648/#2650/#2658 — operator config (extensions.yaml, BREEZE_EXTENSIONS_*) is deliberately absent from .env.example and its only live consumer is the private Workspace extension, so documenting it would expose that surface; build-time extension loading IS deprecated (removal earliest v0.99.0, BREEZE_LEGACY_SOURCE_EXTENSIONS=true to keep) — revisit when the platform launches publicly and features/plugins.mdx likely needs renaming to 'extensions' (it currently documents the SEPARATE, still-mounted /plugins org catalog, which is not wrong). Agent two-phase token rotation #2621/#2647 (internal; /rotate-token is agent-authed, no admin surface); script stdout/stderr/exitCode #2474/#2629 (features/scripts.mdx:100 already documents the correct end state — the fix made reality match the doc); non-superuser migration guard #2626 (CI-only). Linux viewer breeze:// #2614/#2645 — NOT documented because no Linux viewer download is listed anywhere (deploy/binaries.mdx lists macOS .dmg + Windows .msi only); documenting the fix requires first documenting the Linux viewer artifact — LOW-PRIORITY FOLLOW-UP. NO new unconditionally-required env vars in this range (all 9 M365_GRAPH_ACTIONS_* are gated off by default). docsMapping.ts NOT changed — sections were added to existing pages, no new/renamed pages, so help-panel routes and the docs search index are unaffected. CARRIED-OVER GAPS STILL OUTSTANDING (unchanged): v0.94.0 items (portal-user onboarding/invites #2283, partner-policy selectable-org assignment #2280/#2285, ticket-from-AI-conversation #2287, DisplayName->CPE vuln correlation #2290/#2292, internal-OIDC self-host SSO #2295); v0.93.0 deferrals (per-device Vulnerabilities tab reshape #2272, software single-step Add Package + installer URL vars + EDR readiness #2260, quote image-from-URL #2264, advisory Polish-with-AI #2265, native remote-session consent #2254); help-panel route mapping for /backup/profiles still not added to docsMapping.ts. VERIFIED: astro build clean (147 pages); new anchors render as #customer-microsoft-365-graph-actions-write-executor-optional / #who-can-approve / #pending-approvals-survive-a-restart and the ai.mdx->environment.mdx cross-link matches the generated id.
